title: Differences between 10 101 111
wikitext:
[]: Differences between 1.0/1.01/1.11?             Quote Post by ProfessorKaos64 » Mon Apr 19, 2010 11:12 am I know 2.0 is not officially out, I want to buy 1.0 on blu-ray, can someone quickly explain the diff between 1.0, 1.1, and 1.01 ? And Im to understand these are a retelling of the series correct?

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Mon Apr 19, 2010 11:17 am Wrong section, dude.

[]:             Quote Post by ProfessorKaos64 » Mon Apr 19, 2010 11:20 am My apologies, I'm new to the forums so please forgive me :) . Admins , at your leisure please move this thread, Thank you.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 19, 2010 11:59 am 1.0 - Theatrical version. Not available for purchase. 1.01 - DVD version. Some alterations from theatrical version that barely anyone will notice. Really dark, hard to see night scenes. 1.11 - BD version. New scenes added. Picture brightened.

[]:             Quote Post by ProfessorKaos64 » Mon Apr 19, 2010 12:14 pm Sweet , then it appears I'll be geting 1.11, and 2.0 will follow similar number conventions I'm guessing.. Thanks Reichu /thread unless anyone has any other input

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Apr 19, 2010 1:24 pm 1.11 includes 1.01. You want to get 1.11. Your decision, young padawan, is whether to get it on BluRei, DVD, or both... like I did... because I'm hardcore. Are you hardcore?

[]:             Quote Post by ProfessorKaos64 » Mon Apr 19, 2010 4:04 pm It seems you are lol. I'll get blu Rei and ask for the DVD for my bday, problem solved ^__^

[]:             Quote Post by Heromaster111 » Mon Apr 19, 2010 9:08 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: BluRei

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Tue Apr 20, 2010 1:35 am Heromaster111 wrote: I swear I die a little inside everytime I see someone do that.

[]:             Quote Post by KaaVI » Tue Apr 20, 2010 1:50 am Reichu wrote: 1.0 - Theatrical version. Not available for purchase. 1.01 - DVD version. Some alterations from theatrical version that barely anyone will notice. Really dark, hard to see night scenes. 1.11 - BD version. New scenes added. Picture brightened.

[]:             Quote Post by ryatwo » Tue Apr 20, 2010 4:40 am KaaVI wrote: 1.0 is avalable for purchase in australia my evidence is here http://www.ezydvd.com.au/item.zml/806701

[]:             Quote Post by Heromaster111 » Tue Apr 20, 2010 7:56 am BrikHaus wrote: Considering this is EGF, how is it that you are still alive?

[]:             Quote Post by IDontWantNoScrub » Tue Apr 20, 2010 8:18 am Woah. I'm gonna start using that spelling as often as I can.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Apr 20, 2010 8:34 am KaaVI wrote: 1.0 is avalable for purchase in australia my evidence is here http://www.ezydvd.com.au/item.zml/806701

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Apr 20, 2010 9:10 am The Eva Monkey wrote: 1.11 includes 1.01.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 20, 2010 7:23 pm KaaVI wrote: 1.0 is avalable for purchase in australia

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Apr 20, 2010 8:20 pm NAveryW wrote: It does not. It contains a featurette titled "Rebuild of Evangelion 1.01", which is not the movie.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Tue Apr 20, 2010 8:47 pm Reichu wrote: DO do.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Apr 20, 2010 10:28 pm If that AU DVD is by Red Ant, it should be 1.01 or 1.11, just mislabeled. But 1.01 has a 98-minute runtime, not 112 like that AU site says. Is that really 1.11 or something? (Could somebody like Faz confirm or deny what's going on here?)

[]:             Quote Post by KaaVI » Wed Apr 21, 2010 12:05 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: If that AU DVD is by Red Ant, it should be 1.01 or 1.11, just mislabeled. But 1.01 has a 98-minute runtime, not 112 like that AU site says. Is that really 1.11 or something? (Could somebody like Faz confirm or deny what's going on here?)
